programmes.show_description: Thy Fury Spent: A simple case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre.
Missing Joseph: The poisoning of a Lancashire vicar appears to be a tragic accident - until Lynley and Havers delve deeper into his past and discover a sinister motive for murder.
Baby Come Back: More jokes about Essex girls in the popular sitcom. When Sharon meets Dave, the telephone engineer and old flame, her thoughts turn to marriage and motherhood.
Good Heart and Willing Hand: Looming devastation to Five Points forces Corcoran to take drastic, irrevocable action. Injuries old and new push Doctor Matthew Freeman to his limit.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Just Family: Another episode of the classic Essex comedy. Tracey is hurt when Darryl asks her to give up one of her prison visits so that his mum and dad can go and see him.
Homework: Ackland's romantic evening away from Sun Hill spirals into something more sinister when a neighbour appears on her doorstep - carrying a shotgun.
New. Imitation of Life: Daniel and Julia both get a shock at a PCT meeting as he is recognised from his escort work. Vivien tries to prevent an old friend from being conned.
New. The Homecoming: Jimmi and Eva work together to retrieve an abducted baby and stop a woman from taking her own life.
Out of Control: Will Curtis breezes in as the new registrar, and George requires further surgery on the diseased heart given to him, presenting Ed and Tom with yet another moral dilemma.
Eliminate the Negative: Jordan tries to improve the image of the department. Jeff is hailed as a hero, but cannot handle being the centre of attention. Simone's mother antagonises Kirsty.
Homework: Ackland's romantic evening away from Sun Hill spirals into something more sinister when a neighbour appears on her doorstep - carrying a shotgun.
New. Billy Mitchell's love life takes a turn for the better when Carol agrees to go on a date with him, Masood makes a radical decision to prove his innocence.
New. Lucy offers Jane the greatest gift of all, while Becca makes Max a mysterious promise. Zsa Zsa receives disappointing news in the post, but finds an unlikely ally in George.
Against the Odds: The long-running and hugely popular drama series based on the veterinary memoirs of James Herriot. James tries to save a litter of piglets in the snow.
Leo's racing ambition comes to a drastic halt while Ken celebrates a success. Sir Edward gives Tom a business offer he can't refuse.
Reputation, Reputation, Reputation!: Frank and Lu enter the competitive world of hairdressing and find themselves combing through a web of deceit.
Sailing: More laughs with the Essex lasses. The prospect of a happy festive season looks bleak for Tracey and Sharon while their fellas are banged up at Her Majesty's pleasure.
Sinclair and the Wormley Witches: Senior silliness with the sprightly OAPs. Sinclair believes he's the victim of a curse. Truly, Entwistle and Alvin try their hand at exorcism.
Enter the Finger: The record-breaking sitcom that first came to our screens in 1973. Here, Brian Conley plays Barry's fitness-mad neighbour, who outshines him in all things physical.
Will the Genuine Racer Please Stand Up?: The young-at-heart sitcom about a group of retired friends. Howard devises a simple scheme to change his appearance enough to fool even Pearl.
Oysters Kilpatrick: Just as Pearl allows herself a weekend away with Tom, a food critic drops dead in the Whitstable Pearl, leaving her torn between staying or heading back.
Prisoners of the Past: Pearl is surprised to learn that Charlie is back from Canada and living in London, but her search for him leads her to uncover more about Mike's past.
Old Dogs: A fantastic cast of TV legends as the eccentric bunch of ex-policemen. A serial dog killer starts stalking local parks and the team are forced into taking on the case.
Reputation, Reputation, Reputation!: Frank and Lu enter the competitive world of hairdressing and find themselves combing through a web of deceit.
Playing for the Ashes: Superior mystery drama with the two detectives with very different backgrounds. An arson attack results in the death of a star cricketer.
Just Family: Another episode of the classic Essex comedy. Tracey is hurt when Darryl asks her to give up one of her prison visits so that his mum and dad can go and see him.
Beautiful Dreamer: On the move and evading retaliation, Corcoran works to remain hidden while seeing through events he set in motion.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Confidence: Chigwell comedy with the two sisters and their irrepressibly naughty neighbour. Dorien joins the Open University, and Sharon perseveres with her job at the bakery.
Knowing You: Holmes falls out with Proctor when he publicly undermines her.
New. The Hex: Archie encounters a teenage witch who has put a spell on her mother's new boyfriend. However, when he actually becomes ill, no one's more surprised than the girl herself.
New. Crime and Punishments: George discovers Ronnie has deliberately neglected to tell her that the dinner party they are attending is for swingers.
The Eleventh Commandment: Ed breaks the rules to ensure his patient gets a heart transplant, Rosie is frustrated by Mubbs' proposal and Ric decides to resign so he can give Jess support.
Reasons Unknown: On the day of the memorial service for those killed in the college attacks, Ruth struggles to manage the department, while Jeff and Simone both face their demons.
Knowing You: Holmes falls out with Proctor when he publicly undermines her.
New. The Square struggles through crisis together, while Max seeks comfort in an unlikely source. Jane accepts Lucy's offer, while Liz worries when she receives an unexpected gift.
New. Carol is forced to take control as a grief-stricken Max begins to fall apart - but will Becca's scheming throw a spanner in the works? Ian is horrified at Jane and Lucy's news.
Place of Honour: Based on the books by James Herriot, the classic series about his experiences as a Yorkshire vet. Siegfried decides to finally get on Mrs Pumphrey's trout list.
Charles answers to a fraud charge, Leo is left in bad health following his speedboat accident and a glamorous woman arrives in Tarrant.
Best Beware My Sting: A tycoon hires Frank and Lu to keep an eye on his wayward eldest daughter and ensure that her sister's wedding goes off without a hitch.
Getting a Grip: Series two of the most famous sitcom in Essex - isn't it? Sharon and Tracey turn to their man-hungry neighbour Dorien for help when their central heating breaks down.
A Short Introduction to Cooper's Rules: More silver haired shenanigans. Just when PCs Cooper and Walsh have mastered the art of fighting crime, Barry manages to spoil it all.
Is Jeremy Quite Safe?: The gentle comedy that first graced our screens in 1973. A retired jewel thief regales Marina and Davenport with stories of his past exploits in France.
All That Glitters Is Not Elvis: More senior silliness. When Lance tries to convince his wife that Elvis is still alive, Alvin and Entwistle try to help the King make an appearance.
The Queen of Poisons: Part One: When the Mayor dies in the middle of a meeting, Judith, Becks and Suzie uncover lies, scandal and blackmail. Guest starring Peter Davison.
The Queen of Poisons: Part Two: As the suspects' secrets stack up, Judith, Becks and Suzie still can't find anyone with a motive to hurt the Mayor. Guest starring Peter Davison.
Diamond Geezers: Likely Lad James Bolam stars in the quirky series mixing crime with comedy. The team go after the vile Chopper Hadley, back in the country for just one week.
